channel.title: swiftui on Scott's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in swiftui on Scott's Blog

item.title: SwiftUI Localization with Xcode 15
item.description: I need to implement manual language switching in the app settings, which requires additional coding for Swift Localizable.

item.title: How to change the App Theme
item.description: I’m currently developing an App, and I aim to realize multiple theme colors. Users will have the option to switch between themes such as red, orange, blue and others. Therefore, I’m documenting how this functionality is implemented here.

item.title: iOS Widget 06 - Making a Dynamic Configurable Widget for tow Intent
item.description: Demo-06 Download.zip Give users the option to customize their widgets by adding a custom Intent definition to your project. (Developer Documentation)

item.title: iOS Widget 05 - Making a Dynamic Configurable Widget
item.description: Demo-05 Download.zip Give users the option to customize their widgets by adding a custom Intent definition to your project. (Developer Documentation)

item.title: SwiftUI NavigationBar Hidden, Slipe Back Problem
item.description:
1. Navigation Bar Hidden

.navigationBarHidden(true)

In SwiftUI Project when we write this code, the NavigationBar will be hidden, but the slip back will also be invalid. At this time, we can add Navigation Extension.

item.title: SwiftUI Layout - VStack / HStack / ZStack
item.description:
• VStack: A view that arranges its subviews in a vertical line.
  • HStack: A view that arranges its subviews in a horizontal line.
  • ZStack: A view that overlays its subviews, aligning them in both axes.

item.title: SwiftUI Layout - LazyVGrid & LazyHGrid
item.description:
• LazyHGrid Documents
  • LazyVGrid Documents

LazyVGrid and LazyHGrid, one is a vertical grid layout and the other is a horizontal grid layout. The new layout manager of iOS14, like LazyVGrid and LazyHStack with Lazy, indicating that Layout managers will only drawing the child elements when need to be displayed.

item.title: SwiftUI Collection + NavigationStack
item.description:
From Apple Document If your app has a minimum deployment target of iOS 16, iPadOS 16, macOS 13, tvOS 16, or watchOS 9, or later, transition away from using NavigationView. In its place, use NavigationStack and NavigationSplitView instances.

I need a list of Collections, and the custom Cell, then with NavigationStack link to the next View.

Demo Download.zip

item.title: iOS Widget 04 - Widget Clock Refresh
item.description:
I did write a clock widget, but I found that there is a problem every time the clock is refreshed. eg. now is 17:42:25, the widget still is 17:41, maybe after 10 seconds, it can be refreshed with 17:42. I want just 17:43:00, it can be refreshed.

Demo-04 Download.zip

item.title: iOS Widget 03 - Dynamic Launcher Widgets
item.description:
If you want to select a widget bundle based on some logic, example in App has 10 Widgets, but I just want to display two Widgets when man add the Widgets in the Desktop. You can do the following:

Demo-03 Download.zip

item.title: iOS Widget 02 - Making a Configurable Widget - 1
item.description: Demo-02 Download.zip Give users the option to customize their widgets by adding a custom Intent definition to your project.

item.title: iOS Widget 01 - Build Your First iOS Widget
item.description:
Demo-01 Download.zip

Let’s start by building our simple App. Open up Xcode and create a new iOS App Project; make sure SwiftUI is selected in the Interface options and SwiftUI App is selected in the Life Cycle options.

item.title: SwiftUI Animation Scale Image
item.description: How to realize flexible animation of pictures? It’s very simple to use SwiftUI now.

item.title: Why does string localization/internationalization not work in SwiftUI
item.description: It has been proved that if a string in Text(“home) is passed as a text, it can only be interpreted as a localized keyword. If the variable is String type, it doesn’t occur. So the answer is to declare a variable of LocalizedStringKey type.

item.title: How to Use TabView of SwiftUI
item.description:
Setting multiple tabbars in UIKit requires UITabBarController. But it’s implemented through TabView in the SwiftUI.

In this Article we will also introduce how to hide TabBar when view pushed.

Demo Download.zip

item.title: Dynamically Change the AppIcon
item.description:
I found that the AppIcon of Xcode14 is more convenient. So I want to use SwiftUI to test it in the Xcode14.

  • Interface: SwiftUI
  • Language: Swift
  • Xcode: 14
